[TKT-0292] Establish Formal Regression Testing Framework

metadata.resolution: Phase 1 complete. Framework: tests/regression-runner.sh with 44 tests across 5 phases. Final run: 44/44 pass (100%). PG foundation operationalized. PG-down failure tests passed manually. Extensible: add new suites in tests/regression/<name>/, auto-discovered via -p{N} suffix. Reports: state/regression-report-pg-foundation-YYYY-MM-DD.json
metadata.description: Establish a formal, maintainable regression testing framework for the AInchors Nexus platform. Phase 1: Operationalize the 44 tests from the PG Foundation Regression Test Plan (Phase 2 tests) as a re-runnable test suite with pass/fail reporting. Design for extensibility — new test categories are modular additions. Phase 2: Add failure mode tests (PG down/recovery). Phase 3: Add agent/cron integration tests, Notion sync tests, and state transition tests.

tickets.title: [TKT-0293] Expand Regression Testing Framework — Platform-wide Test Coverage

metadata.description:
Following TKT-0292 (44-test PG Foundation suite, 100% pass), expand the regression testing framework to cover all critical platform functions.

AC1: Thrawn to assess the platform and identify critical functions for regression coverage. Deliverable: assessment doc with test scope, test cases, and test criteria for each function.
AC2: Forge assigned as owner of the Regression Testing Framework — build, maintain, operate.
AC3: Forge to build, test, and verify the new test cases identified in AC1.
AC4: Forge to create a dedicated Holocron page for the Regression Testing Framework — live document updated on any framework changes.

tickets.title: [TKT-0294] Fix db-write.sh PG schema mismatch — merge extras into metadata JSONB

metadata.resolution: Option B implemented. db-write.sh now queries PG schema, routes known columns to INSERT, and packs unknown columns into metadata JSONB. Verified: basic columns (ticket.sh pattern), extended fields (sprint/description/etc → metadata), and edge case (id only).
metadata.description: db-write.sh fails PG writes when callers send fields not matching the PG column schema (e.g. sprint, description, resolution). The script generates INSERT with all fields as columns, but PG only has a subset. Unknown columns must be merged into the metadata JSONB column. Fix: modify db-write.sh to query PG schema, separate known columns from unknown, and pack unknowns into metadata.

tickets.title: [TKT-0295] PG Design-to-Implementation Audit — Atlas + Thrawn Joint Review

metadata.resolution: Audit complete. Atlas and Thrawn reports delivered at docs/deliverables/. 7 remediation items: TKT-0298 through TKT-0304.

tickets.title: [TKT-0296] Fix Journal Writer — Real-time append architecture, eliminate session window dependency

metadata.description: Journal writer is broken since ~May 19. Root cause: incremental writer (30-min cron) uses sessions_history(limit=50) to reconstruct entries, but session windows exceed 200+ messages in active days. Earlier entries fall outside the 50-message window and are permanently lost. Result: journals dropped from ~40kB/day to ~2kB/day (90% loss). Blog also broken (cascade failure — reads incomplete journal). Fix: redesign to real-time append architecture where the main session writes journal entries inline during conversation, not as a post-hoc reconstruction. EOD finalizer only adds header + business stream + cost report.

tickets.title: [TKT-0297] Redesign and Redevelop AKB Backlog — Clean Reliable PG-to-Notion Sync

metadata.description: Redesign AKB Backlog PG→Notion sync from scratch. Current: 642 lines across 3 scripts (ticket.sh notion-sync, notion-sync-audit.sh, notion-sync-fix.sh) with constant PG→JSON→Notion drift. New architecture: PG is SSOT. ticket.sh reads from db-read.sh (not JSON file). New pg-to-notion-sync.sh does idempotent PG→Notion sync. Deprecate old audit/fix scripts. Atoms: (1) Audit current drift, (2) Rewrite ticket.sh read path to PG primary, (3) Create pg-to-notion-sync.sh, (4) Wire ticket.sh close/create to trigger sync, (5) Archive old scripts, (6) Update AKB sync cron, (7) Regression test, (8) Close. DB IDs: Backlog=34dc1829-53ff-814b-8257-d3a3bf351d44, Auto-Heal=364c1829-53ff-81c0-9dbd-ff2c907d1a6b, Archive=364c1829-53ff-818e-a783-ebafcb6a9880.

tickets.title: [TKT-0298] TKT-0295-R1: Atomic write pattern verification & hardening

metadata.resolution: All 4 violations fixed: db-write.sh fallback (flock+atomic), owl-track.py (atomic_write_json), owl-alert.py (atomic_write_json), budget-check.sh (Python atomic write). Verified: atomic_write.py self-tests pass, db-write.sh PG path unaffected.

tickets.title: [TKT-0299] TKT-0295-R2: JSON Schema contract for metadata JSONB

metadata.description: Create formal JSON Schema validation layer at db-write.sh boundary for metadata JSONB column. Prevents metadata drift across clients. Atlas P2-required finding (AC1-R2).

tickets.title: [TKT-0300] TKT-0295-R3: Hardcoded state file reference cleanup

metadata.resolution: 5 files fixed: dod-validator.sh (tickets.json->db-read.sh), state-snapshot.sh (4 PG tables -> db-read.sh), gateway-restore.sh (annotated), linkedin-post.sh (atomic write for campaign file + db-read annotation), budget-check.sh (cost-state.json -> db-read.sh with file fallback). All syntax-checked. AC5 scan complete.

tickets.title: [TKT-0301] TKT-0295-R4: Resolve state_v view staleness risk

metadata.resolution: Audit confirmed: all 8 state_v views are regular PostgreSQL views (NOT materialized). Zero staleness risk. Thrawn AC2-E1 is a non-issue — views are live SELECT pass-throughs computed on every read. Added COMMENT documentation to all 8 views for future audit trail.

tickets.title: [TKT-0302] TKT-0295-R5: File fallback race condition guard

metadata.resolution: Already resolved by TKT-0298. db-write.sh fallback path uses flock() exclusive locking: os.open(.lock) -> fcntl.LOCK_EX -> read under lock -> atomic temp->fsync->rename -> LOCK_UN. Two concurrent PG failures will serialize on the lock. No data loss risk.

tickets.title: [TKT-0303] TKT-0295-R6: Add sc_read wrapper for State Checking reads

metadata.description: Complete State Checking coverage: add sc_read wrapper with READ->VALIDATE->VERIFY cycle. Currently direct reads bypass State Checking. Thrawn AC4-G1.

tickets.title: [TKT-0304] TKT-0295-R7: Migrate 3 missing state domains to PG

metadata.description: Cross-reference 8 tables against full Nexus data model. 3 domains potentially missing: model-routing, governance approval, observability correlation. Thrawn AC3-G1.

tickets.title: [TKT-0305] Audit and update all cron payloads to use db-read.sh/db-write.sh — PG operationalisation completion

metadata.description: AC1 (Thrawn) COMPLETE: Audited all 40 active cron payloads for tilde and $HOME references. Aria ROI cron (7a4d8381) was the ONLY instance — fixed via TKT-0306. All 32 isolated-session crons use absolute /Users/ainchorsangiefpl/ paths. 8 main-session crons are safe (~ expands in main sessions). No remaining issues. AC2: Migrate cron file read/writes to PG via db-read.sh/db-write.sh.

tickets.title: [TKT-0306] Fix Aria Weekly Business ROI Summary cron — tilde expansion + PG migration

metadata.description: Arias Weekly Business ROI Summary cron (7a4d8381) failed May 24 with: Write ~/.openclaw/workspace-business/state/business-roi.json failed. Root cause: tilde (~) not expanded in isolated cron session. Fix: replace ~ with full path /Users/ainchorsangiefpl, and migrate business-roi.json write to PG via db-write.sh.

tickets.title: [TKT-0307] Agent RULES.md Foundation Repair — Specialist agents running without procedures

metadata.description:
Agent identity audit (2026-05-26) revealed 11 of 12 agents have no RULES.md in their workspace. The [AGENT]_RULES.md files were created during the RULES.md split (CHG-0421) but were never renamed to RULES.md — agents cannot load them. Impact: all specialist agents (Atlas, Thrawn, Lando, Mon Mothma, Shield, Lex, Sage, Warden, Spark, Aria) are running with only SOUL.md identity and the shared platform RULES.md — missing their specialist procedures, routing rules, and task-specific instructions.

AC1: Fix RULES.md naming — rename [AGENT]_RULES.md → RULES.md in each agent workspace OR create symlinks
AC2: RCA — how did the foundation setup break? CHG-0421 sandbox migration timeline vs RULES.md file timeline
AC3: Fix + Verify — implement and verify each T3 specialist agent loads their specialist procedures, routing rules, and task-specific instructions
AC4: Permanent prevention — ensure this cannot happen again (audit script check for RULES.md presence, agent commissioning checklist update, auto-heal check)

tickets.title: [TKT-0308] CHG: Separate Forge/Krennic/Luthen/Ahsoka to dedicated workspaces

metadata.description: TKT-0307 Atom 3 revealed Forge, Krennic, Luthen, and Ahsoka share Yodas workspace (/Users/ainchorsangiefpl/.openclaw/workspace/). Their SOUL.md and RULES.md files sit in subdirectories (forge/, infra/, etc.) that the platform does not auto-load. They operate as sub-agents with explicit task instructions — missing their specialist identity and procedures. Fix: Separate each into dedicated workspace directories with proper SOUL.md + RULES.md + openclaw.json agent config update.

tickets.title: [TKT-0309] Context Retention Architecture — Memory, TQP Work Resume, Model Drift Survival

metadata.description: AC1 (Atlas) COMPLETE. AC2 (Thrawn) COMPLETE — design approved by Ken 2026-05-26 21:25. AC3 (Forge): Implement Phase 1 — TQP Execution Gate for Yoda inline atoms. Schema: atom_index, state_payload, execution_context, parent_task_id, persistence_type. Auto-resume protocol.

tickets.title: [TKT-0304] TKT-0295-R7: Migrate 3 missing state domains to PG

metadata.description: Cross-reference 8 migrated tables against full Nexus data model. Three domains potentially missing: (1) model-routing state, (2) governance approval state, (3) observability correlation state. Create PG tables and migrate if confirmed. Thrawn finding AC3-G1.

tickets.title: Schema test
Platform Constraint Enforcement - Prevent agents/crons from exceeding system limits
tickets.description: Investigate and produce option paper for systemic solution to prevent agents and crons from exceeding platform limits: model context window overflow, token attention window drift, file size limits, cron timeout thresholds. Symptoms: Stand-up cron tilde-path failure 5x, DOD validator timeout, agent RULES.md exceeding attention window. Options: context budget guards, inline-path reinforcement, prompt truncation at gateway, timeout auto-scaling, file size enforcement. Deliverable: Option paper with recommendation, implementation plan, CHG record.
